results.possibleIdentity: Dr. Myron Leopold Pulier (often referred to as Myron L. Pulier) is a psychiatrist and technical expert with a career spanning over six decades, noted for his early and extensive work integrating computer programming with psychiatry and medical research. He earned his medical degree from the Albert Einstein College of Medicine in 1965 and completed his residency in psychiatry at Hillside Hospital in Glen Oaks, New York, between 1966 and 1969. Since the late 1960s, Dr. Pulier has held staff positions and contributed to pioneering technical projects at various institutions, including Sloan-Kettering/Memorial Hospital, Hillside Hospital, the Nathan Kline Research Institute, and Walter Reed Army Hospital. His technical expertise includes general medicine, psychiatry, and computer programming; notably, he developed a unique programming language and databases for mental health care delivery and designed a computer-controlled electronic tachistoscope for investigating backward masking in schizophrenia. Dr. Pulier is a Clinical Associate Professor at Rutgers New Jersey Medical School and has co-authored textbooks on information technology in mental health. Beyond his medical and technical work, he is a published poet and writer who has characterized computer program development as an artistic process of 'playful reshaping' rather than purely analytic logic. This multidisciplinary background in art, technology, and psychiatry aligns with the goals of the organization Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), with which he has been associated as a technical expert.
